new_classes.label_en: Added Sugar or Sweetening Matter Quality
new_classes.label_de: Qualität des zugesetzten Zuckers oder Süßungsmittels
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a water-based beverage indicating the presence of added sugar or other sweetening matter, distinguishing flavoured/sweetened waters under CN 2202 10 from unsweetened waters under CN 2201.

A quality borne by a water-based beverage indicating the presence of added flavouring substances (natural or artificial), distinguishing flavoured waters under CN 2202 10 from plain waters under CN 2201.

new_classes.label_en: Waters with added sugar, sweetening matter or flavouring (CN 2202 10)
new_classes.label_de: Wasser mit Zuckerzusatz, anderen Süßmitteln oder Aromastoffen (KN 2202 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Waters, including mineral waters and aerated waters, to which sugar, other sweetening matter or flavouring has been added; covering the soft drinks described in paragraph (A) of the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2202. The presence of anti-oxidants, vitamins, stabilisers or quinine does not affect classification here.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three core distinguishing criteria of CN 2202 10 00: (1) the water base (including mineral and aerated waters), expressed via subClassOf SweetenedFlavouredOrAeratedWater which is itself under NonAlcoholicBeverage; (2) the presence of added sugar or sweetening matter, modelled as a quality restriction; and (3) the presence of added flavouring, modelled as a separate quality restriction. The legal note's clarification that anti-oxidants, vitamins, stabilisers or quinine do not disqualify classification is a negative/scope-delimiting statement that cannot be elegantly captured in OWL without a closed-world assumption — this represents the main gap (~15%). The cross-reference to HS EN heading 2202 paragraph (A) (soft drinks) is informational and does not add discriminating criteria beyond those already modelled. The disjunction between sugar-added and flavour-added criteria (either/or suffices) is modelled conservatively with two separate someValuesFrom restrictions rather than a union, which slightly over-restricts but errs on the side of narrowness per engineering rules.
